mnemonics: ARMA Is Like A Car: AR (AutoRegressive) is the engine's momentum, MA (Moving Average) is the road's random shocks.
keyPoints: An ARMA(p, q) model combines an AR(p) model and an MA(q) model into a single equation.
It is used to model time series that exhibit both autocorrelation and the influence of random shocks.
The model is defined as: X_t = c + Σ(φ_i * X_{t-i}) + Σ(θ_i * ε_{t-i}) + ε_t, for i=1 to p and j=1 to q.
commonMistakes: Overfitting the model by choosing values for p and q that are too high, leading to a model that fits the noise rather than the signal.
Failing to ensure the series is stationary before fitting an ARMA model.
Confusing the AR and MA components; AR depends on past values of the series, MA depends on past error terms.
